title: Substack Integrates Pangram Detection Tool, Launches AI Text Recognition Functionality on Web and Mobile
excerpt: Platform-based content community Substack officially launched its AI content detection feature on July 21, 2026, Pacific Time, aiming to enhance platform conten
content_html:
Platform-based content community Substack officially launched its AI content detection feature on July 21, 2026, Pacific Time, aiming to enhance platform content transparency and help readers distinguish between content written by humans and that generated by artificial intelligence. The feature is powered by the AI detection tool Pangram and is now available on the web and iOS app, with an Android version also set to be released soon.

This update covers all scenarios of content on Substack, including articles, notes, replies, and comments. Users can simply click the “…” menu on the corresponding content and select “Scan AI Text,” and the system will display the specific percentages of human-written, AI-generated, and AI-assisted text through a pop-up window provided by Pangram.

It is important to note that this detection tool applies only to texts published after 8:30 AM on July 21 and with paragraphs longer than 100 words. On the underlying technology, Pangram uses a classifier neural network model, whose training dataset was entirely collected before 2021, when generative AI had not yet become widely popular, ensuring the objectivity of the differentiation criteria.

While granting readers the right to know, Substack also retains a high degree of operational autonomy for creators. Creators can self-test or add creation notes during the draft stage, and they can also disable the detection feature completely or remove the mark at any time.

Facing the current situation where the threshold for content production has been lowered by large language models and the proportion of AI-generated content in online texts continues to rise, co-founder and CEO of Substack Chris Best stated that this move aims to address the issue of "mismatch between reader expectations and actual content." This action marks a key step for content platforms in balancing the application of AI tools and maintaining a high-quality human original ecosystem.

sentences.zh: 在底层技术上，Pangram采用了分类器神经网络模型，其训练数据集完全收集于2021年之前，当时生成式AI尚未广泛普及，保证了区分标准的客观性。

sentences.zh: 创作者可以在草稿阶段进行自测或添加创作备注，也可以随时完全禁用检测功能或删除标记。

blocks.html_en: Facing the current situation where the threshold for content production has been lowered by large language models and the proportion of AI-generated content in online texts continues to rise, co-founder and CEO of Substack Chris Best stated that this move aims to address the issue of "mismatch between reader expectations and actual content." This action marks a key step for content platforms in balancing the application of AI tools and maintaining a high-quality human original ecosystem.

sentences.en: Facing the current situation where the threshold for content production has been lowered by large language models and the proportion of AI-generated content in online texts continues to rise, co-founder and CEO of Substack Chris Best stated that this move aims to address the issue of "mismatch between reader expectations and actual content."
sentences.zh: 面对大语言模型降低内容制作门槛、网络文本中人工智能生成内容比例持续上升的现状，Substack联合创始人兼首席执行官Chris Best表示，此举旨在解决“读者期望与实际内容不匹配”的问题。

sentences.en: This action marks a key step for content platforms in balancing the application of AI tools and maintaining a high-quality human original ecosystem.
sentences.zh: 此举标志着内容平台在平衡AI工具应用和维护优质人类原创生态方面迈出了关键一步。

content_html_zh:
基于平台的内容社区Substack于太平洋时间2026年7月21日正式推出AI内容检测功能，旨在增强平台内容透明度，帮助读者区分人类编写的内容和人工智能生成的内容。 该功能由人工智能检测工具 Pangram 提供支持，现已在网络和 iOS 应用程序上提供，Android 版本也即将发布。

本次更新涵盖了Substack内容的所有场景，包括文章、笔记、回复和评论。 用户只需单击“...” 点击相应内容的菜单，选择“扫描AI文本”，系统将通过Pangram提供的弹窗显示人工书写、AI生成和AI辅助文本的具体百分比。

需要注意的是，该检测工具仅适用于7月21日上午8点30分之后发布且段落长度超过100字的文本。 在底层技术上，Pangram采用了分类器神经网络模型，其训练数据集完全收集于2021年之前，当时生成式AI尚未广泛普及，保证了区分标准的客观性。

在赋予读者知情权的同时，Substack也为创作者保留了高度的运营自主权。 创作者可以在草稿阶段进行自测或添加创作备注，也可以随时完全禁用检测功能或删除标记。

面对大语言模型降低内容制作门槛、网络文本中人工智能生成内容比例持续上升的现状，Substack联合创始人兼首席执行官Chris Best表示，此举旨在解决“读者期望与实际内容不匹配”的问题。 此举标志着内容平台在平衡AI工具应用和维护优质人类原创生态方面迈出了关键一步。
